name: Fieldry
mission: The structured field notebook and future research-grade observation capture tool for Waypoint Studio. Private by default — your evidence, your choice to share.
portfolioNote: Core platform instrument — primary place where field observations become useful evidence over time.
strategicRole: Fieldry will implement the Waypoint Observation Standard (WOS) for local capture: date, time, location (with privacy controls), habitat, weather, photos, notes, confidence, verification, and license.
learnToday: Why observation ledgers beat social feeds for naturalists — private first, share only with informed consent
The difference between what you saw, what you measured, and what you think it means
How WOS fields map to a field day: when, where, habitat context, weather, media, and uncertainty
Location privacy: general place names in public copy; precise coordinates only when you choose
wosFields: Location with fuzzing and privacy tiers
Photographs as evidence (not proof of ID alone)
comingSoon: Local field journal aligned with WOS v1
Structured observation form — not a generic notes app
Sketch and photo attachment per record
Export for your archive (Darwin Core–compatible when sharing)
Optional anonymous county-level contribution — never exact harvest or den locations
No accounts required for local capture in v1

preview.title: Your observation ledger
preview.placeholderHint: Date · time · place · habitat · weather · three observations · confidence
preview.description: Fieldry is not a social journal. It is the place where careful observations become evidence — building toward research-grade environmental records without overstating what exists today. Write what you noticed, attach photographs, note uncertainty, and keep coordinates private until you deliberately share.
